forecast_track.position_description: 410 km East Northeast of Itbayat, Batanes

tcws.potential_impact: Minor to moderate threat to life and property.

tcws.areas_raw: Cagayan including Babuyan Islands, Isabela, northeastern Quirino (Maddela), Apayao, northern Abra, Kalinga, eastern Mountain Province, eastern Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, northern Aurora, and northern/central Catanduanes.
tcws.potential_impact: Minimal to minor threat to life and property.

rainfall.risk: Numerous flooding events likely; landslides likely in moderate to highly susceptible areas.

rainfall.areas: Occidental Mindoro, Antique, Negros Occidental, Zamboanga del Sur, Lanao del Norte, and Lanao del Sur
rainfall.risk: Localized flooding possible; landslides possible in highly susceptible areas.
Localized flooding possible; landslides possible in highly susceptible areas.

rainfall.areas: Zambales, Bataan, Cavite, Batangas, Oriental Mindoro, Romblon, Palawan, Aklan, Capiz, Iloilo, Guimaras, Negros Occidental, Negros Oriental, Zamboanga del Sur, Lanao del Norte, and Lanao del Sur
rainfall.risk: Localized flooding possible; landslides possible in highly susceptible areas.

rainfall.risk: Localized flooding possible; landslides possible in highly susceptible areas.

marine.affected_seaboards_raw: Northern and eastern seaboards of Luzon and eastern seaboard of Visayas. Very rough seas up to 6.0 m over Batanes, northern/eastern Babuyan Islands, and northeastern mainland Cagayan; up to 5.0 m over remaining mainland Cagayan/Babuyan Islands and Isabela; up to 4.5 m over Aurora, Camarines Norte, Polillo Islands, Camarines Sur, Catanduanes, eastern Northern Samar, northeastern Eastern Samar, and northern Ilocos Norte.

cpf_risk_matrix.commodity_affected: All dispatch, inter-island/marine-dependent movement, feeds and support trips
cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: TCWS No. 2; gale-force winds; very rough seas up to 6.0 m
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Do not release trips toward Batanes/exposed coastal routes without official clearance and validated safe passage.

cpf_risk_matrix.area_route: Cagayan, Babuyan Islands, Isabela, Apayao, Ilocos Norte, northern Aurora, Catanduanes
cpf_risk_matrix.commodity_affected: Feeds, silo/bulk feeds, live animals, RM transfers
cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: TCWS No. 1; strong winds; exposed coastal/upland route risk
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Validate road passability, wind exposure, receiving readiness, GPS status, and contractor readiness before release.

cpf_risk_matrix.area_route: Northern/eastern seaboards of Luzon and eastern Visayas
cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: Gale Warning No. 4A; rough to very rough seas
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Hold small seacraft/motorbanca-dependent movement; validate larger vessels with Coast Guard, ports, and shipping lines.

cpf_risk_matrix.area_route: Gerona / Tarlac hub and Central Luzon outbound routes
cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: Downstream wind, rain, and marine advisory exposure
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Coordinate route validation for affected corridors; update Affected Delivery Updates file.

dispatch_recommendations.reason: TCWS No. 2 and very rough seas.

dispatch_recommendations.area: Cagayan, Isabela, Ilocos Norte, northern Aurora, Catanduanes and listed TCWS No. 1 areas
dispatch_recommendations.reason: Strong wind and exposed coastal/upland risk.

dispatch_recommendations.area: Northern/eastern Luzon and eastern Visayas seaboards
dispatch_recommendations.reason: Gale Warning No. 4A; rough to very rough seas.

dispatch_recommendations.area: Central Luzon, NCR/CALABARZON, Visayas and Mindanao routes not under hold/suspension
dispatch_recommendations.reason: Habagat/rain/gust exposure and local passability changes.

contractor_advisory: All freight contractors must validate route, weather, road, bridge, and port conditions before dispatch. Keep GPS active, confirm driver/helper readiness, secure cargo, and report flooding, landslides, road closures, port cancellations, low visibility, strong winds, route deviations, vehicle issues, or delivery delays immediately. Do not proceed through unsafe flooded, landslide-prone, wind-exposed, or port-restricted routes without CPF Logistics clearance.
change_log: Updated to PAGASA Tropical Cyclone Bulletin No. 7 issued 11:00 AM, 09 July 2026.
INDAY slightly weakened while moving northwestward over the Philippine Sea.
Maximum sustained winds changed to 165 km/h; gustiness changed to 205 km/h; pressure changed to 945 hPa.
Current center moved to 18.4°N, 129.8°E, 865 km East of Northern Luzon.
Movement changed to northwestward at 15 km/h.
TCWS No. 2 is now in effect over Batanes; TCWS No. 1 remains over listed areas.
Weather Advisory updated to No. 12 and Gale Warning updated to No. 4A.
reliability_notes: PAGASA is the official source of truth.
Satellite imagery is visual context only and must not override PAGASA values.
Forecast cone is not plotted unless official cone geometry is available.
